name: Fresh Oyster Dolmades W/Lemon & Caviar
recipeIngredient: 30 large spinach leaves, stems trimmed and flat
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the water in a 4-quart saucepan.
2. Add the salt and bring to a boil.
3. Add the spinach leaves and cook for 30 seconds.
4. Drain the spinach leaves well.
5. Transfer the leaves to a bowl of ice water (do not tear the leaves; they must remain perfect).
6. Combine the oysters with their liquor in a small saucepan.
7. Add clam juice if needed and simmer over high heat until barely cooked (just beginning to get firm).
8. Drain thoroughly.
9. Spread the spinach leaves out on a towel.
10. Place one oyster off center at the top of each leaf.
11. Season lightly with salt, pepper, and nutmeg.
12. Wrap each oyster by folding the tip of the leaf over and then turning in the opposite sides.
13. Roll the oyster towards the stem to make a neat package.
14. Set aside.
15. Melt the butter in a large skillet over medium heat.
16. Arrange the dolmades, seams down, and heat until just barely warm (3-4 minutes).
17. Transfer to a serving dish.
18. Add the lemon juice to the skillet and heat through.
19. Pour into a small bowl.
20. Stir in the caviar.
21. Spoon the mixture sparingly over the dolmades.
22. Dot each with 1 or 2 pieces of pimento.
23. Garnish with thin slices of lemon.
24. Serve.
